{{Quote|Deesix has gone quiet. I think he's in shock.|Wedge Antilles to Starkiller during the Battle of Kamino|<ref name="Novel">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' novelization]]</ref>}}
'''R2-D6''', or '''Deesix''' as he was referred to, was an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series]] [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] owned by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] prior to and during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
[[File:Repair_droid_TCG_by_Adam_lane.jpg|thumb|left|200px|R2-D6 at work]]
In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], R2-D6 served aboard the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] of Rebel [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] during the [[Kamino uprising]]. During the fighting, Deesix was used as a handhold by [[Subject 1138|Starkiller]], a clone of [[Galen Marek]] who was attempting to infiltrate the [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] [[Timira City Cloning Facility|facilities]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in order to rescue Rebel fighter [[Juno Eclipse/Legends|Juno Eclipse]]. Although nervous about the escalating conflict, Deesix nevertheless stayed true to the current mission and survived the battle.

Deesix was later assigned to a Y-wing on recon duty in the [[Muugrah system]] searching for a pirate base. Though the Y-wing did find the base, it was soon attacked by the pirates and the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] was forced to eject. Deesix became trapped in his socket due to the damage dealt to the ship, and played dead as the Y-wing was tractored aboard a pirate ship, which proceeded to take it to a nearby pirate outpost. Once the outpost commander saw what his men had brought in, he panicked and ordered the fighter disposed of lest he be linked with the Rebel Alliance. The fighter, with Deesix still aboard, was loaded into a cargo container and dumped in [[Squarn's Junkyard]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Muugrah]].

Rewiring his socket to draw power from the Y-wing, Deesix was determined to return to the Alliance with the information he had gathered, and was keen to regroup with the crew of the fighter. He was eventually discovered by a group of Rebels who were looking for spare parts to fix their [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. They managed to free Deesix from his socket with the help of a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Skiff/Legends|skiff]], which they had repurposed as a [[Wikipedia:VTOL|VTOL]] vehicle. Deesix insisted on accompanying them with their searches. While the group continued their search, however, they were attacked by the same group of pirates who had previously sent Deesix to the junkyard, operating on orders to retrieve the droid for [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]]. The fight ended with the Rebels victorious thanks to the help of Squarn's [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] forces, and they soon left the world with Deesix in tow.

Once Deesix was delivered to a Rebel base, the information in his memory was downloaded, saving many Rebel lives from the pirate attacks.
